import type { FeatureId } from \"@calcom/features/flags/config\";\n2: import type { OptInFeaturePolicy, OptInFeatureScope } from \"./types\";\n3: \n4: export interface OptInFeatureConfig {\n5:   slug: FeatureId;\n6:   titleI18nKey: string;\n7:   descriptionI18nKey: string;\n8:   policy: OptInFeaturePolicy;\n9:   /** Scopes where this feature can be configured. Defaults to all scopes if not specified. */\n10:   scope?: OptInFeatureScope[];\n11: }\n12: \n13: /** All available scopes for feature opt-in configuration */\n14: export const ALL_SCOPES: OptInFeatureScope[] = [\"org\", \"team\", \"user\"];\n15: \n16: /**\n17:  * Features that appear in opt-in settings.\n18:  * Add new features here to make them available for user/team opt-in.\n19:  */\n20: export const OPT_IN_FEATURES: OptInFeatureConfig[] = [\n21:   // Example - to be populated with actual features\n22:   // {\n23:   //   slug: \"bookings-v3\",\n24:   //   titleI18nKey: \"bookings_v3_title\",\n25:   //   descriptionI18nKey: \"bookings_v3_description\",\n26:   //   policy: \"permissive\",\n27:   //   scope: [\"org\", \"team\", \"user\"], // Optional: defaults to all scopes if not specified\n28:   // },\n29: ];\n30: \n31: /**\n32:  * Get the configuration for a specific opt-in feature by slug.\n33:  */\n34: export function getOptInFeatureConfig(slug: string): OptInFeatureConfig | undefined {\n35:   return OPT_IN_FEATURES.find((f) => f.slug === slug);\n36: }\n37: \n38: /**\n39:  * Check if a slug is in the opt-in allowlist.\n40:  * Acts as a type guard, narrowing the slug to FeatureId when true.\n41:  */\n42: export function isOptInFeature(slug: string): slug is FeatureId {\n43:   return OPT_IN_FEATURES.some((f) => f.slug === slug);\n44: }\n45: \n46: /**\n47:  * Check if there are any opt-in features available.\n48:  */\n49: export const HAS_OPT_IN_FEATURES: boolean = OPT_IN_FEATURES.length > 0;\n50: \n51: /** Whether there are opt-in features available for the user scope */\n52: export const HAS_USER_OPT_IN_FEATURES: boolean = OPT_IN_FEATURES.some((f) => !f.scope || f.scope.includes(\"user\"));\n53: \n54: /** Whether there are opt-in features available for the team scope */\n55: export const HAS_TEAM_OPT_IN_FEATURES: boolean = OPT_IN_FEATURES.some((f) => !f.scope || f.scope.includes(\"team\"));\n56: \n57: /** Whether there are opt-in features available for the org scope */\n58: export const HAS_ORG_OPT_IN_FEATURES: boolean = OPT_IN_FEATURES.some((f) => !f.scope || f.scope.includes(\"org\"));\n59: \n60: /**\n61:  * Get opt-in features that are available for a specific scope.\n62:  * Features without a scope field are available for all scopes.\n63:  */\n64: export function getOptInFeaturesForScope(scope: OptInFeatureScope): OptInFeatureConfig[] {\n65:   return OPT_IN_FEATURES.filter((f) => !f.scope || f.scope.includes(scope));\n66: }\n67: \n68: /**\n69:  * Check if a feature is allowed for a specific scope.\n70:  * Features without a scope field are allowed for all scopes.\n71:  * Features not in the config are NOT allowed (must be explicitly configured).\n72:  */\n73: export function config.scope.includes(scope);\n77: }\n78: \\\"@calcom/features/flags/features.repository\\\";\\n8: import { MembershipRepository } from \\\"@calcom/features/membership/repositories/MembershipRepository\\\";\\n9: import { prisma } from const featuresRepository = new FeaturesRepository(prisma);\\n21: const teamRepository = new TeamRepository(prisma);\\n22: \\n23: /**\\n24:  * Helper to get user's org and team IDs from their memberships.\\n25:  * Returns orgId (if user belongs to an org) and teamIds (non-org teams).\\n26:  */\\n27: async function getUserOrgAndTeamIds(userId: number): Promise<{ orgId: number | null; null = null;\\n34:   const teamIds: number[] = [];\\n35: \\n36:   for (const membership of memberships) {\\n37:     if (membership.team.isOrganization) {\\n38:       orgId = membership.teamId;\\n39:     } else {\\n40:       teamIds.push(membership.teamId);\\n41:     }\\n42:   }\\n43: \\n44:   return { orgId, teamIds };\\n45: }\\n46: \\n47: export const featureOptInRouter = router({\\n48:   /**\\n49:    * Get all opt-in features with states for current user.\\n50:    * This considers all teams/orgs the user belongs to.\\n51:    */\\n52:   listForUser: authedProcedure.query(async ({ ctx }) => {\\n53:     const { orgId, teamIds } = await getUserOrgAndTeamIds(ctx.user.id);\\n54: \\n55:     return featureOptInService.listFeaturesForUser({\\n56:       userId: ctx.user.id,\\n57:       orgId,\\n58:       teamIds,\\n59:     });\\n60:   }),\\n61: \\n62:   /**\\n63:    * Get all opt-in features with states for a team settings page.\\n64:    * Used by team admins to configure feature opt-in for their team.\\n65:    * Also returns the organization state if the team belongs to an organization.\\n66:    */\\n67:   listForTeam: createTeamPbacProcedure(\\\"featureOptIn.read\\\").query(async ({ input }) => {\\n68:     // Get the team's parent organization ID (if any)\\n69:     const parentOrg = await teamRepository.findParentOrganizationByTeamId(input.teamId);\\n70:     const parentOrgId = parentOrg?.id ?? null;\\n71: \\n72:     return featureOptInService.listFeaturesForTeam({ teamId: input.teamId, parentOrgId, scope: \\\"team\\\" });\\n73:   }),\\n74: \\n75:   /**\\n76:    * Get all opt-in features with states for organization settings page.\\n77:    * Used by org admins to configure feature opt-in for their organization.\\n78:    */\\n79:   listForOrganization: createOrgPbacProcedure(\\\"featureOptIn.read\\\").query(async ({ ctx }) => {\\n80:     // Organizations use the same listFeaturesForTeam since they're stored in TeamFeatures\\n81:     // Pass scope: \\\"org\\\" to filter features that are scoped to organizations\\n82:     return featureOptInService.listFeaturesForTeam({ teamId: ctx.organizationId, scope: \\\"org\\\" });\\n83:   }),\\n84: \\n85:   /**\\n86:    * Set user's feature state.\\n87:    */\\n88:   setUserState: const teamRepository = new TeamRepository(prisma);\n22: \n23: /**\n24:  * Helper to get user's org and team IDs from their memberships.\n25:  * Returns orgId (if user belongs to an org) and teamIds (non-org teams).\n26:  */\n27: async function getUserOrgAndTeamIds(userId: number): Promise<{ orgId: number | null; teamIds: number[] }> {\n28:   const memberships = await MembershipRepository.findAllByUserId({\n29:     userId,\n30:     filters: { accepted: true },\n31:   });\n32: \n33:   let orgId: number | null = null;\n34:   const teamIds: number[] = [];\n35: \n36:   for (const membership of memberships) {\n37:     if (membership.team.isOrganization) {\n38:       orgId = membership.teamId;\n39:     } else {\n40:       teamIds.push(membership.teamId);\n41:     }\n42:   }\n43: \n44:   return { orgId, teamIds };\n45: }\n46: \n47: export const featureOptInRouter = router({\n48:   /**\n49:    * Get all opt-in features with states for current user.\n50:    * \\\"@calcom/features/flags/features.repository\\\";\\n7: import { prisma } from \\\"@calcom/prisma\\\";\\n8: \\n9: import type { IFeatureOptInService } from \\\"./IFeatureOptInService\\\";\\n10: vi.mock(\\\"../config\\\", async (importOriginal) => {\\n14:   const actual = await importOriginal<typeof import(\\\"../config\\\")>();\\n15:   return {\\n16:     ...actual,\\n17:     isFeatureAllowedForScope: () => true,\\n18:   };\\n19: });\\n20: \\n21: // Helper to generate unique identifiers per test\\n22: const uniqueId = () => [];\\n26: \\n27: afterEach(async () => {\\n28:   for (const cleanup of cleanupFunctions) {\\n29:     await cleanup();\\n30:   }\\n31:   cleanupFunctions.length = 0;\\n32: });\\n33: \\n34: // Access \\n39: interface TestEntities {\\n40:   user: { id: number };\\n41:   org: { id: number };\\n42:   team: { id: number };\\n43:   team2: { id: number };\\n44:   featuresRepository: FeaturesRepository;\\n45:   service: IFeatureOptInService;\\n46:   createdFeatures: string[];\\n47:   setupFeature: (enabled?: boolean) => Promise<FeatureId>;\\n48: }\\n49: \\n50: /**\\n51:  * Creates isolated test entities (user, org, teams) and returns them along with a cleanup function.\\n52:  * Each test gets its own entities, ensuring complete isolation even when tests run in parallel.\\n53:  */\\n54: async function setup(): Promise<TestEntities> {\\n55:   const id = uniqueId();\\n56:   const createdFeatures: string[] = [];\\n57: \\n58:   // Create test user\\n59:   const user = await prisma.user.create({\\n60:     data: {\\n61:       email: `test-opt-in-${id}@example.com`,\\n62:       username: `test-opt-in-${id}`,\\n63:     },\\n64:   });\\n65: \\n66:   // Create test org\\n67:   const org = await prisma.team.create({\\n68:     data: {\\n69:       name: `Test OptIn Org ${id}`,\\n70:       slug: `test-opt-in-org-${id}`,\\n71:       isOrganization: true,\\n72:     },\\n73:   });\\n74: \\n75:   // Create test teams\\n76:   const team = await prisma.team.create({\\n77:     data: {\\n78:       name: `Test OptIn Team ${id}`,\\n79:       slug: `test-opt-in-team-${id}`,\\n80:       parentId: org.id,\\n81:     },\\n82:   });\\n83: \\n84:   const team2 = await prisma.team.create({\\n85:     data: {\\n86:       name: `Test OptIn Team 2 ${id}`,\\n87:       slug: `test-opt-in-team-2-${id}`,\\n88:       parentId: org.id,\\n89:     },\\n90:   });\\n91: \\n92:   const featuresRepository = getFeaturesRepository();\\n93:   const service = getFeatureOptInService();\\n94: \\n95:   // Helper to create a feature for a test and track it for cleanup\\n96:   const setupFeature = async (enabled = true): Promise<FeatureId> => {\\n97:     const featureSlug = `test-opt-in-feature-${uniqueId()}` as FeatureId;\\n98:     createdFeatures.push(featureSlug);\\n99:     await prisma.feature.create({\\n100:       data: {\\n101:         slug: featureSlug,\\n102:         enabled,\\n103:         type: \\\"EXPERIMENT\\\",\\n104:       },\\n105:     });\\n106:     // Clear cache after creating feature so getAllFeatures() returns fresh data\\n107:     clearFeaturesCache(featuresRepository);\\n108:     return featureSlug;\\n109:   };\\n110: \\n111:   // Cleanup function - automatically registered with afterEach\\n112:   const cleanup = async () => {\\n113:     // Clean up in correct order to respect foreign key constraints\\n114:     await prisma.userFeatures.deleteMany({\\n115:       where: { userId: user.id },\\n116:     });\\n117:     await prisma.teamFeatures.deleteMany({\\n118:       where: { teamId: { in: [team.id, team2.id, org.id] } },\\n119:     });\\n120:     await prisma.feature.deleteMany({\\n121:       where: { slug: { in: createdFeatures } },\\n122:     });\\n123:     await prisma.membership.deleteMany({\\n124:       where: { userId: user.id },\\n125:     });\\n126:     await prisma.team.deleteMany({\\n127:       where: { id: { in: [team.id, team2.id] } },\\n128:     });\\n129:     await prisma.team.deleteMany({\\n130:       where: { id: org.id },\\n131:     });\\n132:     await prisma.user.delete({\\n133:       where: { id: user.id },\\n134:     });\\n135:   };\\n136: \\n137:   // Register cleanup to run automatically after each test\\n138:   cleanupFunctions.push(cleanup);\\n139: \\n140:   return {\\n141:     user,\\n142:     org,\\n143:     team,\\n144:     team2,\\n145:     featuresRepository,\\n146:     service,\\n147:     createdFeatures,\\n148:     setupFeature,\\n149:   };\\n150: }\\n151: \\n152: describe(\\\"FeatureOptInService Integration Tests\\\", () => {\\n153:   describe(\\\"resolveFeatureStatesAcrossTeams\\\", \"@calcom/features/flags/features.repository\";\n7: import { prisma } from \"@calcom/prisma\";\n8: \n9: import type { IFeatureOptInService } from \"./IFeatureOptInService\";\n10: \n11: // Mock isFeatureAllowedForScope to always return true for integration tests.\n12: // The scope validation logic is tested in unit tests; integration tests focus on database behavior.\n13: vi.mock(\"../config\", async (importOriginal) => {\n14:   const actual = await importOriginal<typeof import(\"../config\")>();\n15:   return {\n16:     ...actual,\n17:     isFeatureAllowedForScope: () => true,\n18:   };\n19: });\n20: \n21: // Helper to generate unique identifiers per test\n22: const uniqueId = () => `${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 9)}`;\n23: \n24: // Store cleanup functions to ensure they run even if tests fail\n25: const cleanupFunctions: Array<() => Promise<void>> = [];\n26: \n27: afterEach(async () => {\n28:   for (const cleanup of cleanupFunctions) {\n29:     await cleanup();\n30:   }\n31:   cleanupFunctions.length = 0;\n32: });\n33: \n34: // Access private clearCache method through type assertion\n35: const clearFeaturesCache = (repo: FeaturesRepository) => {\n36:   (repo as unknown as { clearCache: () => void }).clearCache();\n37: };\n38: \n39: interface TestEntities {\n40:   user: { id: number };\n41:   org: { id: number };\n42:   team: { id: number };\n43:   team2: { id: number };\n44:   featuresRepository: FeaturesRepository;\n45:   service: IFeatureOptInService;\n46:   createdFeatures: string[];\n47:   setupFeature: (enabled?: boolean) => Promise<FeatureId>;\n48: }\n49: \n50: /**\n51:  * Creates isolated test entities (user, org, teams) and returns them along with a cleanup function.\n52:  * Each test gets its own entities, ensuring complete isolation even when tests run in parallel.\n53:  */\n54: async function setup(): Promise<TestEntities> {\n55:   const id = uniqueId();\n56:   const createdFeatures: string[] = [];\n57: \n58:   // Create test user\n59:   const user = await prisma.user.create({\n60:     data: {\n61:       email: `test-opt-in-${id}@example.com`,\n62:       username: `test-opt-in-${id}`,\n63:     },\n64:   });\n65: \n66:   // Create test org\n67:   const org = await prisma.team.create({\n68:     data: {\n69:       name: `Test OptIn Org ${id}`,\n70:       slug: `test-opt-in-org-${id}`,\n71:       isOrganization: true,\n72:     },\n73:   });\n74: \n75:   // Create test teams\n76:   const team = await prisma.team.create({\n77:     data: {\n78:       name: `Test OptIn Team ${id}`,\n79:       slug: `test-opt-in-team-${id}`,\n80:       parentId: org.id,\n81:     },\n82:   });\n83: \n84:   const team2 = await prisma.team.create({\n85:     data: {\n86:       name: `Test OptIn Team 2 ${id}`,\n87:       slug: `test-opt-in-team-2-${id}`,\n88:       parentId: org.id,\n89:     },\n90:   });\n91: \n92:   const featuresRepository = getFeaturesRepository();\n93:   const service = getFeatureOptInService();\n94: \n95:   // Helper to create a feature for a test and track it for cleanup\n96:   const setupFeature = async (enabled = true): Promise<FeatureId> => {\n97:     const featureSlug = `test-opt-in-feature-${uniqueId()}` as FeatureId;\n98:     createdFeatures.push(featureSlug);\n99:     await prisma.feature.create({\n100:       data: {\n101:         slug: featureSlug,\n102:         enabled,\n103:         type: \"EXPERIMENT\",\n104:       },\n105:     });\n106:     // Clear cache after creating feature so getAllFeatures() returns fresh data\n107:     clearFeaturesCache(featuresRepository);\n108:     return featureSlug;\n109:   };\n110: \n111:   // Cleanup function - automatically registered with afterEach\n112:   const cleanup = async () => {\n113:     // Clean up in correct order to respect foreign key constraints\n114:     await prisma.userFeatures.deleteMany({\n115:       where: { userId: user.id },\n116:     });\n117:     await prisma.teamFeatures.deleteMany({\n118:       where: { teamId: { in: [team.id, team2.id, org.id] } },\n119:     });\n120:     await prisma.feature.deleteMany({\n121:       where: { slug: { in: createdFeatures } },\n122:     });\n123:     await prisma.membership.deleteMany({\n124:       where: { userId: user.id },\n125:     });\n126:     await prisma.team.deleteMany({\n127:       where: { When false, the feature is disabled for everyone.\n45:    * When true, the feature is NOT necessarily enabled - enablement still depends on\n46:    * the org, team, and user state hierarchy.\n47:    */\n48:   globalEnabled: boolean;\n49:   orgState: FeatureState;\n50:   teamStates: FeatureState[];\n51:   userState: FeatureState;\n52:   /**\n53:    * Policy that determines how feature opt-in states are evaluated.\n54:    */\n55:   policy: OptInFeaturePolicy;\n56: }): EffectiveStateResult {\n57:   // Derive all conditions upfront\n58:   const orgEnabled = orgState === \"enabled\";\n59:   const orgDisabled = orgState === \"disabled\";\n60:   const anyTeamEnabled = teamStates.some((s) => s === \"enabled\");\n61:   const anyTeamDisabled = teamStates.some((s) => s === \"disabled\");\n62:   const allTeamsDisabled = teamStates.length > 0 && teamStates.every((s) => s === \"disabled\");\n63:   const userEnabled = userState === \"enabled\";\n64:   const userDisabled = userState === \"disabled\";\n65: \n66:   // Explicit enablement exists above user level\n67:   const hasExplicitEnablementAboveUser = orgEnabled || anyTeamEnabled;\n68: \n69:   // Check conditions in order of precedence and return with reason\n70:   if (!globalEnabled) {\n71:     return { enabled: false, reason: \"feature_global_disabled\" };\n72:   }\n73: \n74:   if (orgDisabled) {\n75:     return { enabled: false, reason: \"feature_org_disabled\" };\n76:   }\n77: \n78:   if (policy === \"strict\") {\n79:     // Strict policy: ANY explicit disable blocks\n80:     if (anyTeamDisabled) {\n81:       return { enabled: false, reason: \"feature_any_team_disabled\" };\n82:     }\n83: \n84:     // Strict policy: User opt-in alone is NOT enough - requires explicit enable from org/team\n85:     if (userEnabled && !hasExplicitEnablementAboveUser) {\n86:       return { enabled: false, reason: \"feature_user_only_not_allowed\" };\n87:     }\n88: \n89:     if (userDisabled) {\n90:       return { enabled: false, reason: \"feature_user_disabled\" };\n91:     }\n92: \n93:     if (!hasExplicitEnablementAboveUser) {\n94:       return { enabled: false, reason: \"feature_no_explicit_enablement\" };\n95:     }\n96: \n97:     return { enabled: true, reason: \"feature_enabled\" };\n98:   }\n99: \n100:   // Permissive policy (default): disables only win if ALL teams disable\n101:   if (allTeamsDisabled) {\n102:     return { enabled: false, reason: \"feature_all_teams_disabled\" };\n103:   }\n104: \n105:   if (userDisabled) {\n106:     return { enabled: false, reason: \"feature_user_disabled\" };\n107:   }\n108: \n109:   if (!userEnabled && !hasExplicitEnablementAboveUser) {\n110:     return { enabled: false, reason: \"feature_no_explicit_enablement\" };\n111:   }\n112: \n113:   return { enabled: true, reason: \"feature_enabled\" };\n114: }\n115: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/cal.com--4b99072b@4b99072bcc01e7a252df828db71248a202cb65d1/-/blob/packages/features/feature-opt-in/lib/computeEffectiveState.ts"}}}

The `setTeamState` endpoint checks `isOptInFeature`, but there's no scope validation at the router level, even though the service validates it downstream and throws an error if it fails.\n\nThe issue is that if the service throws `ErrorWithCode` and the tRPC middleware doesn't properly convert it to a `TRPCError`, the client gets an `INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR` instead of a `BAD_REQUEST`—which would be a compliance violation. The router should validate scope directly and throw `TRPCError` itself rather than relying on the service layer to handle it.\n\nBut without seeing the middleware that handles `ErrorWithCode` conversion, I can't say for certain if this is actually broken. {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_011Y5tXGd6gWBpNAfjhWaz91","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The review has been written to `/workspace/review.json`. Here's a summary of the defects found:\n\n## Review Summary\n\nAll three defects are in `packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/featureOptIn/_router.ts`. They share the same root cause pattern:\n\n### Defect Pattern: Unconditional `assignedBy` in mutation handlers\n\n**Affected mutations:** `setUserState` (line 103), `setTeamState` (line 131), `setOrganizationState` (line 161)\n\n**Root cause:** Each mutation handler calls the service with `assignedBy: ctx.user.id` unconditionally, regardless of whether `state` is `\"inherit\"`, `\"enabled\"`, or `\"disabled\"`. The service interface (`IFeatureOptInService`) uses a **discriminated union** where:\n\n- The `\"enabled\" | \"disabled\"` branch **requires** `assignedBy: number`\n- The `\"inherit\"` branch **must NOT have** `assignedBy`\n\nSince `input.state` is typed as `\"enabled\" | \"disabled\" | \"inherit\"` (the full enum), TypeScript cannot resolve the call to either union branch — producing a compile error.\n\n**Fix:** Branch on `input.state === \"inherit\"` before calling the service, passing `assignedBy` only in the non-inherit branch. This correctly satisfies the discriminated union type and matches the service contract."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1645,"cache_read_input_tokens":92443,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1645,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"81abd80b-1ee3-415f-b796-9cbdd0be29e9","uuid":"c7257191-c4b4-40d9-9cfe-f43b3834fa6c"}

Here's a summary of the defects found:\n\n## Review Summary\n\nAll three defects are in `packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/featureOptIn/_router.ts`. They share the same root cause pattern:\n\n### Defect Pattern: Unconditional `assignedBy` in mutation handlers\n\n**Affected mutations:** `setUserState` (line 103), `setTeamState` (line 131), `setOrganizationState` (line 161)\n\n**Root cause:** Each mutation handler calls the service with `assignedBy: ctx.user.id` unconditionally, regardless of whether `state` is `\"inherit\"`, `\"enabled\"`, or `\"disabled\"`. The service interface (`IFeatureOptInService`) uses a **discriminated union** where:\n\n- The `\"enabled\" | \"disabled\"` branch **requires** `assignedBy: number`\n- The `\"inherit\"` branch **must NOT have** `assignedBy`\n\nSince `input.state` is typed as `\"enabled\" | \"disabled\" | \"inherit\"` (the full enum), TypeScript cannot resolve the call to either union branch — producing a compile error.\n\n**Fix:** Branch on `input.state === \"inherit\"` before calling the service, passing `assignedBy` only in the non-inherit branch.
